details.text: The Battle of Buçaco happened on September 27, 1810. It was during the Peninsular War. This war was in the mountains of Portugal. In this battle, Lord Wellington's army won. The army was made of British and Portuguese soldiers. The French army was very big. They attacked Wellington's army many times. The French did not know where the soldiers were. Wellington put his men in a tricky spot. They were hidden on the back side of a hill. So, the French could not see them well. The fighting was very hard. The French forces failed. They had to run away. They lost many men. Before this, the French tried to attack Wellington's spot. But later, Wellington had to move. He went to the Lines of Torres Vedras. The French wanted to push the British out of Portugal. The French first attacked a place called Ciudad Rodrigo. Then, the French tried to get to Lisbon.

It was important to wait. The French needed to wait. This was because the defenses around Lisbon were being built. These defenses were called the Lines of Torres Vedras. Wellington made things hard for the French. He broke bridges and roads. This made it hard for the French to move. It slowed down their walk. By the end of September, the French found Wellington's army. The army was on a high place called the ridge of Bussaco. This ridge was a very good spot to defend. It was on the way to Coimbra and then to Lisbon. Wellington had many soldiers. He had six British walking groups. The Portuguese army also helped. They had two groups of soldiers. They also had three other groups of soldiers. Four British groups of riders were there. Four groups of Portuguese riders were also there. Six groups of guns were set up. These guns were from Britain, Germany, and Portugal. The whole army was 50,000 people. Half of them were Portuguese. The French army was 60,000 people. They had different groups of soldiers. One group was under Reynier. Another group was led by Ney. A third group was under Junot. They also had a reserve group of riders.

Wellington put his army on a high hill called Bussaco Ridge. He faced the east. He told his officers to cut a road along the back side of the ridge. This road helped them talk to each other. Cole was on the left side. Craufurd, Spencer, Picton, and Leith were next. Hill was on the right side. Masséna thought he had many more men than the British. He wanted to attack the British army. He did not want to go around them. Masséna sent men to look at the steep ridge. The British soldiers stayed on the back slope. They were told not to make fire. Masséna planned to send Reynier to the middle of the ridge. He thought this spot was the British right side. If the II Corps attack seemed to work, Masséna would send Ney's group. They would attack along the main road. The VIII Corps stayed behind the VI Corps. Ney said he was ready to attack. But Reynier changed his mind. He thought his attack would fail. Some people say the French officers were not sure. They thought it was hard to tell their men to climb up. They did not want to fight an enemy up top. Some say Junot, Reynier, and Ney all wanted to attack right away. Others say they all did not want to attack. It seems most true that the officers were not sure.

In the early morning mist, Sir John Fortescue Reynier's soldiers attacked. Heudelet sent his best group up the hill. When the first soldiers reached the top, they saw the 74th Foot and two Portuguese groups. There were also 12 cannons. The French tried to change how they stood. They tried to stand in a line. But the friends shot very hard with muskets. Soon, the French soldiers got mixed up. Still, they held on to a small spot on the hill. Further away, Merle's group came up the hill. Picton quickly gathered his fighters near the top road. The French tried to line up again. But the shots from all sides hit them hard. So, the French ran down the hill. Merle got hurt. Another leader, Jean François Graindorge, was badly hurt. Wellington rode to Colonel Alexander Wallace. He said, "Wallace, I have never seen a braver charge." When Heudelet's second group stopped at the bottom, Reynier asked Foy to attack right away. The friends attacked a weak spot. The French hit a Portuguese group. They beat them and ran away. But the mist went away. Now, no enemies were seen on the right side of the British. Wellington told Leith to move his men north to help Picton. Before Foy's men could rest.

When they heard gunshots, Ney thought Reynier's men were winning. So, he told them to attack. The main road went up a hill past small towns like Moura and Sula. It went to the top near the Convent of Bussaco. Loison's group fought hard against strong British soldiers. Near the top, 1,800 men waited. When Loison's group got near the convent, two British groups stood up. They shot many bullets very close. Then, they ran forward with their sharp sticks (bayonets). The French group fell down and ran away. Their leader, BG Édouard Simon, was hurt and caught. Later, Loison's second group met shooting from two cannons and some English and Portuguese guns. This group also ran away. A last push by another French group was stopped by a Portuguese group. Both sides kept fighting all day. But the French did not try to attack hard again. The French lost 522 men who died. 3,612 men were hurt. 364 men were caught. The friends' group lost 200 dead. 1,001 were hurt. 51 were lost. The British and Portuguese each lost 626 men. Masséna saw how big Wellington's army was. He saw his strong spot. So, that afternoon, he sent riders to check the ends of the hill. They looked for a way around. The French army went back toward Mortagoa. They lit fires in the woods to hide where they went. On the night of September 28, Massena told his army to go down the Sardaõ road by Boialvo. This way went around the other side.

Wellington moved his army. He went to the Lines of Torres Vedras. He got there well by October 10. Masséna kept moving forward. He left his sick soldiers at Coimbra. The Portuguese took these soldiers a few days later. This was a big fight in the Peninsular War. The Portuguese army fought well. They won the fight. This win made the new soldiers feel much better. Later, they fought at the Battle of Sobral on October 14. Masséna saw they were too strong. So, he went away for the winter. He did not have food for his men. The British and Portuguese attacked them often. Masséna lost many men. Some were caught. Others died from hunger or sickness. He went back to Spain in 1811. This freed Portugal from the French. Only one fort, Almeida, was still held by the French. During this going away, they fought at the Battle of Sabugal.

Stewartby was a very big place that made bricks. It was the biggest brick factory in the whole world. The owners, Hanson, had to close it in 2008. This was because they could not follow the rules for smoke. The factory had four chimneys. At first, people thought they would keep the chimneys for history. But later, they took down the chimneys on September 26, 2021. This brickworks had the world's biggest oven, called a kiln. It made 500 million bricks when it worked the best. A company called BJ Forder & Son started making bricks in 1897. They moved their place to Wootton Pillinge in 1920. Then, in 1937, they called it Stewartby for the Stewart family. The company grew big. It even had its own ambulance and fire team. They used a special clay called Lower Oxford Clay. This clay was from the sea a long, long time ago. This clay helped them burn less coal.

The Stewartby Brickworks stopped in May 2008. People thought the four tall chimneys were not safe. So, they took them down on September 26, 2021. Later, in December 2023, a company called Universal Destinations & Experiences bought 480 acres of land. They want to build a fun park there. They think this place is good because it is near London and Luton Airport. The village has a train station. This village was once planned for a big science place. But that plan stopped in June 2015. Now, there is a new house building called Hanson's Reach. They will build 750 new houses there. They also plan to build a big trash burner in an old clay pit. Kimberley College opened here in September 2013. There is also a small school called Broadmead Lower School. Another school is Marston Vale. The village has a football team since 1999. Also, a group called Stewartby Amateur Operatic and Dramatic Society puts on shows. They do this since 1951.

In Stewartby, there is a big park. This park has a new slide. It also has a zip wire. People plan to make the park bigger. Behind the club house, they built a BMX track. The train line in Stewartby opened in 1905. It was part of the Varsity Line. This line went from Oxford to Cambridge. They plan to open old parts of the line. This is part of the East West Rail scheme. Now, trains run every hour. They run from Bedford to Bletchley. London Northwestern Railway runs these trains. They use Class 230 trains. The lake has the Stewartby Water Sports Club. This club helps people with fishing. It also helps with boat racing, sailing, wakeboarding, and waterskiing. You can also swim in open water. You can paddleboard, canoe, or wind-surf there. Michaela Tabb is a referee for snooker and pool. She is from Scotland. She helped many women referees. She started in pool. She judged big games. She judged at the WPA World Nine-ball Championship. She judged at the Mosconi Cup. She got ready to referee the World Snooker Tour in 2001. She was the top woman referee for 14 years. She was the first woman to judge a big snooker tournament in 2002. She judged the final in 2007. She was the first woman to judge the World Snooker Championship final. She did this twice. This was in 2009 and 2012.

Imagine a magic change happening inside a hard, shiny thing. This change is called a displacive transformation. It is a change in the crystal structure of a solid. This change does not need atoms to move far away. Instead, the atoms just wiggle a little bit. They move small steps. These steps are smaller than the space between neighbors. The atoms stay in their general spots. They keep their group shape. A good example is the martensitic transformation. This happens in steel. Someone first named it for a hard part in steel. This part forms when steel cools very fast. Later, people found that other things can do this too. This includes things that are not iron. So, the word "martensite" now means any product from this kind of change. This change is a teamwork move. It is a smooth, even change.

Scientists watch tiny bits called atoms. When many atoms move in a certain way, it can change something. Some call this a "military transformation." This is different from changes that happen slowly, like in normal life. Charles Frank and John Wyrill Christian first talked about this. A common change is the "martensitic transformation." This one is very studied. It is just one small group of these changes. The change in steel is a big example of this. But, new things are also becoming important. For example, "shape memory alloys" are gaining notice. A change happens when atoms move and push their neighbors. This moving makes the shape change. This is called a "displacive transformation." These changes are very big. So, people made more ways to talk about these changes. This helps us understand them better.

We can look at two kinds of changes. One is from things called lattice-distortive strains. The other is from things called shuffles. Homogeneous lattice-distortive strains are also called Bain strains. These strains change one Bravais lattice into a different one. A strain matrix S helps show this. This matrix changes one vector, called y, into a new vector, called x. This is homogeneous. It means that straight lines become new straight lines. Examples are when a cubic lattice gets bigger on all three sides (dilation). Or when it shears into a monoclinic structure. Shuffles are different. Shuffles mean the very small moving of atoms inside the unit cell. Pure shuffles usually do not change the shape of the unit cell. Instead, they mostly change its symmetry and how it is built.

When things change their shape, a new line forms. This line separates the old stuff from the new stuff. Making this new line costs some energy. This energy cost depends on how well the two parts fit together. Another energy cost happens when the shape changes a lot. If the new shape is stuck by the old stuff, it might stretch or squeeze. This stretching or squeezing makes a strain energy. The mix of these line energies and strain energies changes how fast the change happens. It also changes how the new material looks. For some changes, like "shuffle transformations," the line energy is the most important. This is different from other changes. In those other changes, the strain energy is bigger.

Imagine a crystal structure. This structure can change its shape. We can look at how it changes in two ways. One is called "dilation." The other is called "shear." If the change is mostly from "shear," we can find a line that does not change its shape. This line is not distorted. But, if the change is mostly from "dilation," all the lines will get stretched or squished. We look at the "shear-dominated transformations." We check the "strain energies." These energies are compared to the natural wiggles of the atoms. This tells us if the energy makes a big difference. If the energy is a big deal, the change is called "martensitic." If the energy is not a big deal, the change is called "quasi-martensitic."

Austenite and martensitic steels are very similar. Austenite has a shape called face-centered cubic (FCC). When it changes to martensite, the shape changes too. It becomes a body-centered tetragonal shape (BCT). This change happens because of a process. In this process, carbon atoms do not have enough time to move out. So, the shape gets a little longer in one way. It also gets a little shorter in the other two ways. Even though the shapes are different, the way the atoms stick together is still much the same. This change makes the steel much harder. Martensite has extra carbon atoms inside it. Because of this, it gets stronger. This is like when you bend metal many times. The small flaws stop the atoms from moving easily. This makes the material very hard.

Scientists found a new way that things can change. This change is a mix of two things. One is a "displacive sublattice transition." The other is "atomic diffusion." They used a special machine called a high-pressure X-ray diffraction system to see this. They named this new change the "pseudo martensitic transformation." Also, they told us about Donn Fulton Eisele. He was an officer in the United States Air Force. He was also a test pilot. Later, he was a NASA astronaut. In 1968, he flew on the Apollo 7 mission. After he left NASA and the Air Force in 1972, he helped people in Thailand. He did this for the Peace Corps. He was born on June 23, 1930. He was born in Columbus, Ohio.

Eisele got a job. So, he went to learn how to fly. He trained at some bases. These bases were Goodfellow Air Force Base in Texas. He also trained at Williams Air Force Base in Arizona. And he trained at Tyndall Air Force Base in Florida. In 1954, he got his wings to be a pilot. After that, he flew for four years. He flew in South Dakota. He also flew in Libya until 1958. Later, in 1962, he went to a special school. This school was the U.S. Air Force Experimental Flight Test Pilot School. It was at Edwards Air Force Base in California. Some friends were there too. Eisele worked as an engineer. He was also a test pilot. He worked at the Air Force Special Weapons Center. This was at Kirtland Air Force Base in New Mexico. He flew planes for special weapon tests. He flew for a long time. He flew over 4,200 hours. Of those hours, 3,600 were in jet planes.

Eisele was in a group of brave space travelers. This group was part of NASA. They were picked in October 1963. In early 1966, Eisele was picked to be a pilot. He was for the Apollo 1 team. Other pilots were "Gus" Grissom and Ed White. But Eisele hurt his shoulder. He hurt it two times during practice. So, someone else named Roger B. Chaffee took his spot. Later, after getting surgery, Eisele was picked again. This time, it was for the second Apollo trip. His team had Walter "Wally" Schirra and Walter Cunningham. At this time, Eisele got a bigger job. He became the Senior Pilot. In December 1966, the Apollo 2 trip was stopped. People said it was not needed. So, Schirra, Eisele, and Cunningham waited as backup. They were backup for Grissom's team. But then, Grissom, White, and Chaffee died. They died in a fire on January 27, 1967.

Eisele might not fly the rocket. His secret love life was a problem. He was having a secret affair. This woman would later be his second wife. Deke Slayton, the boss, warned the crew. He said they could be "expendable." He also warned them. He said they must keep their secrets private. Eisele stayed on the team. So, on October 11, 1968, Apollo 7 flew. It was an 11-day trip. This was a first test flight for a new kind of American rocket ship. At this time, the job title "Senior Pilot" changed. It became "Command Module Pilot." Eisele worked with Schirra and Cunningham. They practiced moving parts. They practiced docking with the top part of the Saturn IB rocket. Eisele also acted as the guide. He looked at the stars. He helped line up the rocket's guide system. The team also did eight successful engine tests. They tested the engine on the service module.

In July 1972, Eisele worked for the U.S. Peace Corps in Thailand. He was the Country Director there. Two years later, he left Thailand. Then, he became the Sales Manager for Marion Power Shovel. This was part of Dresser Industries. After that, Eisele worked with Oppenheimer & Company. He handled money accounts for private people and big companies. In 1980, Eisele moved to Wilton Manors, Florida. In 1981, Eisele got a job on the Wilton Manors City Commission. He worked in that job for about one year. After Eisele passed away, the City of Wilton Manors named Donn Eisele Park after him. In 1986, Eisele guided Comet Chase flights. These flights left Miami and New York City. In 1987, Eisele died. He was 57 years old. He died from a heart attack. He was on a work trip to Tokyo, Japan. He was going to see a new Space Camp opening there.

Eisele was a very good person. He was an Eagle Scout. He was also in groups like Tau Beta Pi and Freemason. He belonged to a place called Luther B. Turner Lodge # 732 in Columbus, Ohio. During his life, he got many awards. Some awards were the NASA Exceptional Service Medal. He also got Air Force Senior Pilot Astronaut Wings. He got the Air Force Distinguished Flying Cross. He shared an award from AIAA in 1969. He also got a special award from the National Academy of Television Arts and Sciences in 1969. Eisele was with a team of Apollo astronauts. They went into the International Space Hall of Fame in 1983. He was one of 24 Apollo astronauts. They went into the U.S. Astronaut Hall of Fame in 1997. In 2008, NASA gave him an award even after he passed away. This was for his Apollo 7 mission.

A book tells the story of Donn Eisele. This book is about his life. It came out in 2007. Another book, called Apollo Pilot, also tells his story. This book came out later, in 2017. John Mese acted as Donn Eisele in a TV show in 1998. Ryan Doom also acted as him in a TV show in 2015. Susan Eisele Black gave a piece of Moon rock. She gave it to the Broward County Main Library. This was on October 23, 2007. The library is in Fort Lauderdale, Florida. It is the only library in the USA that has a Moon rock to show. These special Moon rocks are usually at science museums or in schools.

Bharatpur I is a place. It is a small area for helping the community. It is in Murshidabad district. This district is in the state of West Bengal, India. Bharatpur is at a certain spot on the map. It has neighbors. Kandi CD block is to the north. Beldanga II CD block is to the east. Bharatpur II CD block and Ketugram I CD block are to the south. Burwan CD block is to the west. Bharatpur I CD block is in the Mayurakshi Dwaraka plain. This plain is in the south-east part of the Rarh region. The Bhagirathi River cuts the district in two. One side is called Rarh. The other side is called Bagri. The Rarh region is bumpy. It has soil made of clay and laterite.

The Rarh area is in the west part of a place. Water flows there. This water comes from the Bhagirathi river. The Bhagirathi comes from the hills in a place called Santhal Pargana. This place is in Jharkhand. The Farakka Barrage controls the water flow. It lets water go into the Bhagirathi through a canal. After that, water also comes from the Mayurakshi system. A large area near Kandi town gets water from many rivers. These rivers are the Mayurakshi, Dwarka, Brahmani, Gambhira, Kopai, and Bakreshwar. The Mayurakshi river gives the most water. Some other western spots also get water. Bharatpur I CD block is an area of 183.72 km2. It has one panchayat samity. It has eight gram panchayats. It has 121 village councils. It has 92 mouzas. It has 82 villages with people. The Bharatpur police station helps this block. The main office for this block is in Bharatpur.

There are several small village groups called Gram panchayats in Bharatpur I block. These are Alugram, Amlai, Bharatpur, Godda, Gundiria, Jajan, Sijgram, and Talgram. Jajan gram panchayet is in the north-west part of Bharatpur block. This panchayet has ten villages. Their names are Kujura, Palitpara, Akta, Harishchandrapur, Sardanga, Bindarpur, Jadavpur, Ruha, Jajan, and Jadupur. In 2011, the whole Bharatpur I CD block had 172,702 people. All these people lived in villages. There were 89,088 men. There were 83,614 women. In 2001, the total people were 150,839. There were 78,238 men. There were 72,601 women. The number of people grew a lot. This growth was 16.38 percent between 1991 and 2001.

In a place called Bharatpur I CD block, the number of people grew a lot. This growth happened between the years 2001 and 2011. The growth was 14.45 percent. There were many big villages there. Some big villages were Jajan and Harishchandrapur. Others were Bindarpur and Talgram. Bharatpur village was very big. Some other villages were Sijgram and Saiyad Kulutia. The 2011 count showed many people could read and write. The total number of people who were literate was 94,247. This was for people who were six years old or older. Of these people, 52,620 were boys. And 41,627 were girls. The difference between the learning rates for girls and boys was 10.37 percent.

In 2011, people in Bharatpur I CD block were counted. Muslims were 99,217 people. This number was 57.45% of all people. Hindus were 73,214 people. This was 42.39% of the people. Other groups had 271 people. This was a very small part, only 0.16%. In two places, Bharatpur I and Bharatpur II, something changed. For Muslims, the part of the people got bigger. It went from 51.93% in 1991 to 55.13% in 2001. For Hindus, the part got smaller. It went from 48.06% in 1991 to 44.801% in 2001. In Murshidabad district, in 2011, Muslims were 4,707,573 people. They were 66.27% of the people. Hindus were 2,359,061 people. They were 33.21% of the people. Other people were 37,173 people. This was 0.52%. For Muslims in this district, their part got bigger. It went from 61.40% in 1991 to 63.67% in 2001. For Hindus, their part got smaller. It went from 38.39% in 1991 to 35.92% in 2001.

Bengali is the main language. Almost everyone speaks it. In West Bengal, the poor in the countryside were a big problem. Murshidabad district had a high number of poor people in the village. Other places like Purulia and Bankura also had high poverty. These numbers came from old counts. In a place called Bharatpur I CD block in 2011, many people worked in farming. About 23% were farmers who owned land. A big group, 46.61%, were farm helpers. Small groups worked in homes or other jobs. There are 82 villages in Bharatpur I CD block. All 82 villages have electricity. Most villages, 81 villages, have clean drinking water. Many villages, 23 villages, have post offices. Almost all villages, 96.34%, have phones. Some villages have good roads. Many villages also have ways to travel, like buses or trains. Only a few villages, 5 villages, are for farming.

Long ago, starting in 1977, big changes happened to the land in West Bengal. The government took extra land. Then, it gave this land to the farmers. These land changes changed who owned the land. In 2013–14, we looked at the people who farmed in Bharatpur I CD block. Some people were bargadars. There were 3,831 of them. Some people had a patta, which is a paper paper showing land right. There were 4,642 people with a patta. Small farmers had land between 1 and 2 hectares. There were 3,823 small farmers. Marginal farmers had very little land, up to 1 hectare. There were 16,887 marginal farmers. The most people were agricultural labourers. There were 27,020 labourers. The block had many shops and stores for farming. In 2013–14, the block grew a lot of food. They grew 96,620 tonnes of Aman paddy. This was the main winter crop. They grew 23,571 tonnes of Boro paddy. This was the spring crop. They also grew 966 tonnes of wheat. They grew 4,613 tonnes of potatoes. Lastly, they grew 12,060 tonnes of sugar cane.

In the years 2013 to 2014, a place called Bharatpur I CD block used a lot of water for farming. The total farm area with water was 5,220 hectares. Canal water helped farm 2,000 hectares. Tank water helped farm 1,125 hectares. Deep tube wells helped farm 40 hectares. Other ways helped farm 433 hectares. Murshidabad is famous for silk work. This is true since a long time ago. The silk work has three main parts. First, it is growing mulberry plants and raising silkworms. Second, it is peeling the raw silk. Third, it is weaving the silk cloth. Carving from ivory was also an important small job. This was popular when the Nawabs ruled. Khagra and Jiaganj are big places for this. They send 99% of the ivory crafts to other countries. Now, carving with sandalwood is more popular than ivory carving. People make many metal and brass dishes in Khagra, Berhampore, Kandi, and Jangipur. Making beedi (small cigarettes) is very popular in the Jangipur area.

In Bharatpur I CD block, there are 82 villages. Some villages do not have a school. Thirty villages have more than one small school. Thirty-five villages have at least one small school and one middle school. Fifteen villages have at least one middle school and one big school. In 2014, the area had three small health centers. These centers had 35 beds. There were also 8 doctors there. The block had 24 small health help places. People went to these places for care. Seven thousand six hundred twenty-eight people stayed in the hospital. One hundred sixty-four thousand six hundred three people came for outside care. The block has three main places for health care. One is the Bharatpur Rural Hospital. It has 30 beds. Another is the Amlai Primary Health Centre. It has 4 beds. The last one is the Jajan PHC. It has 10 beds.

Bharatpur I CD block is a place. It is in the Murshidabad district. Here, the water underground has some arsenic. This arsenic is a little bit bad. The WHO says that the water should have only 10 mg/litre of arsenic. The Indian Standard says it can have up to 50 mg/litre. Most blocks in Murshidabad have arsenic that is higher than the WHO level. Almost all blocks have arsenic that is higher than the Indian Standard level. Also, 17 blocks have very high arsenic, above 300 mg/litre. In Bharatpur I CD block, the most arsenic found was 82 mg/litre. A different person was Emily Rosaline Orme. She lived from 1835 to 1915. She was from Scotland. She was a fighter for women's rights. She led a group called the Edinburgh National Society for Women's Suffrage.

Orme was born in the year 1835. Her parents were Eliza Andrews and Charles Orme. They were a rich family. They made beer and strong drinks. When she was little, she lived in London. She met important artists. These artists were part of a group called the Pre-Raphaelite movement. Some artists were Holman Hunt and the Rossettis. She also met David Masson. A helper at the British Museum showed her to him. This helper was named Coventry Patmore. Patmore was also a poet. He married her aunt. Her aunt's name was Emily Augusta Patmore. Emily was also part of this group. She wrote a poem. The poem was called The Angel in the House. This poem talked about women staying at home. It also talked about men working outside the home.

Orme got married to Masson on August 27, 1853. They first lived in Orme's parents' house. They shared friends with many interests. These interests were about politics, art, and writing. A smart thinker named John Stuart Mill visited them there. He also spoke about women's right to vote in Edinburgh later. The couple moved to Edinburgh in 1865. This was when her husband got a big job. He taught about speaking skills and English at the University of Edinburgh. He really liked women to get an education at the university. He was one of the first teachers to talk to women at the Edinburgh Ladies' Education Association. He also supported women getting medical learning. They lived first at Rosebury Crescent. Then, they lived at 10 Regent Terrace from 1869 to 1882. After that, they moved to Great King Street. Finally, they lived in Lockharton Gardens.

A man was born to a successful notary. His mother was a woman from a lower class. He was taught art in Florence. A painter named Andrea del Verrocchio taught him. He started his work in the city. Then, he worked for a man named Ludovico Sforza in Milan. Later, he worked again in Florence. He also worked in Milan. He even worked for a short time in Rome. Many people wanted to copy his art. These people were his students. King Francis I invited him to France. He spent his last three years in France. He passed away in 1519. Since he died, people still love his work. His great art and his smart ideas keep people interested. He is still remembered a lot in art and culture.

Brittany talked about a new movie. She thought about it a lot. It came up often with her friends and work friends. She felt she needed a good plan for it. She asked Grace what she thought. She also asked if Grace had seen a TV show. Grace thought about it. She said yes. She went through something like this last spring. She spent much time looking at things. She also compared different ways to do it. Then, she picked a plan that worked. It was not easy at the start. But when she began, things got better by themselves. Grace told Brittany to start small. She said to build up her know-how and feeling good slowly. The most important thing is not to think too much. She said to just take the first step. That step is usually the hardest part. Brittany nodded her head. She agreed with Grace.

Deborah asked a question. She asked about recycling habits. She read about it online last night. She found many new ideas. She said that living green has many ways. Each way has good things and bad things. Calvin said yes. He knows about it. His friend Jason tried being eco-friendly a few months ago. He had a good time. They said that being steady is important. They also said that being patient is key. Results do not come right away. It needs hard work. It needs to learn from mistakes. Deborah thought this was good to know. She was worried before. She was not sure if she could do it.

Electric cars are very talked about now. Many people and experts talk about them. A smart person named Sarah White talked about this. She said that electric cars change how we talk to each other. Some studies in Alexisfurt show this. People buy electric cars much more now. Big companies are spending lots of money on electric car places. They think this will make things run better. Some people worry about this fast change. They say it leaves people behind. The talk does not stop. New things come out every day. These things make us think of new ideas. People want to know how this changes their day. This is about technology and how we live. It is a very fun thing to learn about.

Abraham Lincoln was born on February 12, 1809. He was born in a small cabin in Kentucky. He grew up on Sinking Spring Farm near Hodgenville, Kentucky. His parents were Thomas Lincoln and Nancy Hanks Lincoln. Abraham was a descendant of Samuel Lincoln. Samuel Lincoln was an Englishman. He came to Massachusetts in 1637. His family also came from the Harrison family in Virginia. His grandfather was Captain Abraham Lincoln. The captain moved the family from Virginia to Kentucky. Sadly, the captain died in a raid by Native Americans in 1786. The family lived in Hardin County, Kentucky, in the early 1800s. Thomas and Nancy got married on June 12, 1806. After marrying, they moved to Elizabethtown, Kentucky. They had three children. Their names were Sarah, Abraham, and Thomas Jr. Thomas Jr. passed away when he was a baby.

If she wins the tournament, her rank will jump a lot. She will go from 114th to number 14. This is what the WTA says. Her money in the bank will also get bigger. Staying healthy is very important now. People want to feel good in body and mind. Stephen Gibson is a famous coach. He lives in Harrisshire. He made a big plan for health. They do not want to wait until they are sick.

Lincoln did not like the war with Mexico. This war was from 1846 to 1848. Lincoln thought President James K. Polk had a bad reason for the war. He thought Polk wanted the two countries to fight. Lincoln thought Polk wanted to look good. He wanted glory. Lincoln liked the Wilmot Proviso. This was a plan from 1846. It wanted to stop slavery in lands from Mexico. Polk said the Mexican soldiers started the war. He said they came into Texas. He said they hurt American people on American land. In 1847, Lincoln asked Polk to tell Congress the exact place. He asked for the "spot." But the Polk group did not answer. This made Lincoln lose friends in his area. The papers made fun of him. They called him "spotty Lincoln."

A group in South Africa is taking a big step. This group is a human rights helper. They are taking a case in a court. They are suing the South African government. They want to stop sending weapons to the United States. They say this might break the local rules. They also worry about world peace and safety. The Southern Africa Litigation Centre, or SALC, said this on Tuesday. SALC filed a paper in the North Gauteng High Court. They want to stop or change the papers for arms exports. These papers were given by the NCACC. SALC is a law group. It helps people with rights in Southern Africa. SALC argues that the NCACC did not follow the rules. These rules are in the National Conventional Arms Control Act.

She said, "It is like a dream. I do not know what is happening. I do not know what to say. I am sorry." She asked to enjoy this moment now. Chwalinska played well. She passed three hard rounds. Virtual reality is a big talk now. Many people talk about it. Experts and regular people care about it. A smart person named Sarah Tran talked about it. Maja Chwalinska from Poland was a tennis player. She reached a big final. This was a Grand Slam singles final. She beat Diana Shnaider from Russia. The score was 7-6 (4) and 6-4. Chwalinska is 24 years old. She has a chance to be great. She can be like Emma Raducanu. Raducanu did this at the 2021 US Open. On Saturday, she plays Mirra Andreeva. This is at Roland Garros. Chwalinska and Raducanu are special. They are the only ones to do this. This is for men and women. They reached a big final. This was from the qualifying rounds. This has happened since 1968. After that, she sat down. She breathed very hard.

The Silk Road gets its name from silk. Silk was first made in China. This trade of silk helped connect many trade paths. These paths went across big lands. A man named Ferdinand von Richthofen first made the name popular. He went to China many times. He did this between 1868 and 1872. But people used the name before that time. Sometimes, people also call it "Silk Route." Even though the name was made in the 1800s, many people did not know about it then. It became popular later. A Swedish map maker named Sven Hedin wrote a book about it. He wrote this book in 1938.

Long ago, after the year 1453, a big group called the Ottoman Empire started to fight with other big groups. They wanted to control the land paths for trade. This made European countries look for other ways to trade. This gave Europe more power with their trade friends. This time started a new time. It was called the Age of Discovery. It also started European colonies. Later, people started connecting the whole world more. Today, people call it the "New Silk Road." This name is for big building plans on old trade paths. Two big plans are famous. One is the Eurasian Land Bridge. Another is the Chinese Belt and Road Initiative, or BRI. UNESCO named parts of the old Silk Road as special places in 2014. Another part was named in 2023. Other paths are still being looked at.

The Silk Road was a very old path. It was used for a long time. Many big things happened there. For example, there was the Black Death. There were also the Mongol conquests. This road was not controlled by one group. It was spread out everywhere. So, it was not always safe. People traveling there faced big dangers. They could meet robbers or wild groups. The land was also very hard to cross. Not many people went the whole way. They stopped at small towns. They used other people to help them. More than just things, people shared ideas. They shared religion, like Buddhism. They shared smart thoughts and science. Different groups mixed these ideas along the way. Many kinds of people used these paths. Sadly, sicknesses like the plague also traveled on this road. This might have helped the Black Death spread.

That first step is usually the hardest part. Nicholas nodded. He said, "That makes sense."

In 1831, Lincoln's father moved the family to a new farm. This farm was in Coles County, Illinois. After this, Abraham went to live alone. He made his home in New Salem, Illinois. He lived there for six years. During 1831 and 1832, Lincoln worked at a general store. The store sold many things. He became known for being strong and brave. He did this after he won a wrestling game. He wrestled with a leader of some rough boys. These boys were called the Clary's Grove boys. In 1832, he wanted to be a candidate for the Illinois House of Representatives. But, he stopped his campaign. He went to serve as a captain in the Illinois Militia. This was during the Black Hawk War. He was elected the captain of his group. But, he did not fight in battle. When he ran for office, Lincoln spoke about better ways to travel on the Sangamon River. He made big crowds listen to him. But, he did not have a famous name. He did not have strong friends. He also did not have much money.

A-DNA is a special shape of DNA. You see this shape when water is taken away. It is shorter and wider than B-DNA. RNA can also take this double spiral shape. Most times, RNA and DNA together make an A-form. But, sometimes, they make a B-form too. A-DNA has a deep, narrow part that proteins cannot easily reach. However, it has a wide, shallow part. This part lets proteins come close. But, it does not give as much secret information as the deep part. A-DNA likes to be in places with less water. The base pairs in A-DNA are tilted. They are not straight with the spiral line. People first thought A-DNA was just a lab trick. Now, we know A-DNA has many jobs in nature.

Lincoln had four boys. The first boy was Robert Todd Lincoln. He was born in 1843. He was the only child who lived to grow up. Another boy was Edward Baker "Eddie" Lincoln. He was born in 1846. Eddie died in 1850. He might have had tuberculosis. The third boy was William Wallace "Willie" Lincoln. He was born in 1850. Willie died in 1862. He died from a fever at the White House. The last boy was Thomas "Tad" Lincoln. He was born in 1853. Tad died in 1871. He died from edema. Lincoln loved children very much. The Lincolns were not too strict with their own children. Eddie and Willie dying made the parents feel much sadness. Lincoln felt "melancholy". This is now thought to be clinical depression.

Little gadgets you wear are very popular now. These are called wearable devices. Many grown-up experts talk about them. A smart person named Angela Edwards talked about this. She said these gadgets change how we play with friends. Studies in Lake Sandraview showed this. People use these gadgets more each year. Big companies are spending lots of money on them. They think these gadgets can make work easier. They also think they can make us work better. But, some people worry. They say that change is too fast. Some people cannot keep up with it. New things come out every day. This keeps the talk going. People want to know how this affects their daily life. This is about technology and us. It is a very fun thing to learn about.

A long time ago, there was a way to trade. This way was called the Silk Road. It was a path of trade in Asia. It was used from a long time ago until the middle of the 1400s. This road was very long. It covered over 6,400 kilometers on land. This road was very important. It helped people trade goods. It also helped cultures mix. It helped different beliefs meet. The name "Silk Road" came later. Some smart people now like to call it "Silk Routes." They say this name is better. This is because the routes were a big, tricky web. This web connected many places. These places were in Asia. They were also near Africa and Europe. Some smart people even say the name is wrong. They think the old stories only talked about big, strong kingdoms. They think these stories forgot about other people's helpful things.

Eric asked a question. He asked about product reviews. He read about it online last night. He found new ideas. He said there are many ways for return policy. Each way has good things and bad things. Roberto said yes. He knows about it. His friend Teresa tried delivery. She had a good time. Teresa said the key is being steady and patient. This is because results do not come fast. It needs hard work. It needs wanting to learn from mistakes. Roberto thinks this is a good lesson. This lesson is for people thinking about it. Eric said this is helpful. He was worried before. He was not sure if it was okay.

A long time ago, there was a big fight between two big groups. These groups were the United States (US) and the Soviet Union (USSR). The US was with the capitalist Western Bloc. The USSR was with the communist Eastern Bloc. This fight was called the Cold War. It started after the Second World War. It stopped when the Soviet Union broke apart in 1991. It was called "cold" because the two big groups did not fight each other directly. But they still fought in other places. These were called proxy wars. They supported different sides there. They fought over ideas and money. They also had a race to make more weapons. These were regular weapons and big, scary nuclear weapons. The fight also included things like the Space Race. They also used secrets and made false stories, which is called propaganda. They also used trade stops, called embargoes. And sometimes, they even used sports to show who was better.

The Silk Road got its name from lovely silk cloth. This silk came mostly from China. The trade path started when the Han dynasty grew big. This was around 202 BCE to 220 CE. A man named Zhang Qian explored. He traveled to Central Asia. He brought this area under one rule. The Chinese cared a lot about keeping their trade safe. So, they made the Great Wall of China. This wall kept the trade road safe. The Parthian Empire was very important. It linked the road to the Mediterranean Sea. The Roman Empire grew in the west. This made the trade system bigger. By the first century CE, people in Rome, Egypt, and Greece wanted Chinese silk. Other good things from the East were tea, colors for cloth, sweet smells, and fine plates called porcelain.

There are special parts in DNA and RNA called bases. Two bases are adenine and guanine. These are purines. Cytosine, thymine, and uracil are pyrimidines. The way these bases stick to sugar is different. For purines, the bond forms at the 9 nitrogen. For pyrimidines, the bond forms at the 1 nitrogen. Also, a phosphate group connects to the sugar. This makes a bond. This bond is called an ester bond. The sugar part is deoxyribose. The DNA and RNA get their color from oxygen and nitrogen atoms in their backbone. Nucleic acids are made when little parts called nucleotides join together. They join through bonds between the 5' and 3' carbon atoms. The order of these nucleotides is the sequence. This sequence is like letters in a word. For DNA, the sequence can be GACT. For RNA, the sequence can be GACU.

Stacy asked John a question. She asked about weddings. She read about it on the computer last night. She found things that were new to her. She said there are many ways to celebrate holidays. Each way has good things and not-so-good things. John said yes. He knows about it. His friend Erica tried a family get-together. It was a good time for her. They said that being steady and patient is very important. This is because good things do not always show right away. It needs hard work. It also needs wanting to learn from mistakes. John thinks that is a good lesson for anyone. Stacy said that is helpful. She felt scared. She was not sure if it would work for her.

Z-DNA is a special, rare shape for DNA. It is like a left-handed spiral. This shape can form inside living things. But, we do not know what it does. It looks longer and skinnier than normal DNA shapes. The main opening on Z-DNA is not really a groove. It has a small opening on the other side. This shape likes it when there is a lot of salt. Some changes in the DNA bases can make this happen. The G part of the DNA bonds to the 5' PO part. This makes it hard for protons to swap. Z-DNA base pairs stand almost straight up from the spiral. It does not have single base pairs. Instead, it has a GpC repeat. The GpC stack has good overlap. The CpG stack has less overlap. The zigzag look of Z-DNA comes from this.

DNA has a special shape. This shape is called secondary structure. It is about how parts of the DNA strands stick to each other. In DNA, there are two strands. These two strands hold together with little bonds called hydrogen bonds. The parts on one strand match up with parts on the other strand. This sticking together makes the shape of the DNA. The bases in DNA are purines and pyrimidines. Purines are adenine and guanine. They have a double ring. Pyrimidines are cytosine and thymine. They have a single ring. A purine base always pairs with a pyrimidine base. Guanine (G) pairs with cytosine (C). Adenine (A) pairs with thymine (T) or uracil (U). The secondary structure of DNA mostly comes from these two strands wrapping around and pairing up.

Littlehempston is a small village. It is in a place called Devon, in England. This village has many old, pretty houses. People call it Little Hempston. Some people also call it Hempston Arundel. The village has a church. The church is up on a little hill. Near the church, a path starts. This path takes walkers through fields and woods. It goes for two miles to a town called Totnes. The village has two places to eat. One is called The Tally Ho. It is in the middle of the village. The other is called The Pig & Whistle. It is near the road to Totnes. Also, there is a phone box. There is a noticeboard too. They are next to the post box. This area is in front of a small stream. The stream goes to a big river. This river is called the River Dart.

Zimmerman showed a new version of a song. This was in April 2011. He showed it on Ustream. The song was not finished then. He said it was a song made ten years before. The new song used a sound from the first song. The first song came out in 2001. They played the song live in May 2011. They played it again in August 2011. This was during the Meowingtons Hax Tour. The song came out for everyone on November 4, 2011. In December 2011, the song was in a show called Hard x Mouth Taped Shut. Also, the song was on a book of songs in 2014. This book was called 5 Years of Mau5. A song named "Aural Psynapse" from the 2001 song was in a game. The game was called Little Rocketman.

Yannis Kotsiras is a singer from Greece. He wrote his own songs too. He was born in Athens. His birthday was October 5, 1969. He started singing in 1990. He sang folk music. His first album came out in 1996. It was called Athoos Enochos. He made his first live album in 2002. In 2004, he sang a song called "Pass the Flame." This song was for the Athens Olympic Games. He sang it in English and Greek. In 2010, he started working with Yasmin Levy. Some of his best songs are "Filakas Aggelos" and "Anathema Se." Joseph Masson was a businessman. He was from Canada. He is thought to be the first rich French Canadian man. He lived from 1791 to 1847.

Masson was a big businessman in Canada. This was in the 1830s. He was born in Saint-Eustache, Quebec. He was born in the year 1791. His name was Masson. His father was Antoine Masson. His father was a joiner. He did not know how to write. His mother was Suzanne. She was named Pfeiffer or Payfer. Masson went to school in Saint-Eustache. Then, when he was 16, he went to Saint-Benoît, Mirabel. There, he learned about business. He learned from a man named Duncan McGillis. Later, a man named Hugh Robertson hired him. Hugh Robertson was a Scottish merchant. He thought Masson was very smart. The company had some hard times. It had a bankruptcy in 1814. But Masson showed he was good. He was also a leader in many groups. He was the leader of the Terrebonne group. He was also a leader of the City Gas.

Masson got married in 1818. His wife's name was Marie-Geneviève-Sophie Raymond. She was the daughter of Jean-Baptiste Raymond. He was a lord of Lake Matapedia. Masson worked very hard on his business. He wanted to beat his other business rivals. His import-export companies grew bigger. They also did many different things. In 1830, he had three big companies. One was in Glasgow. It was led by Hugh Robertson. Another was in Montreal. It was led by Masson. The third was in Quebec. It was made by Masson. Masson and Robertson owned most of the money in these companies. Masson was the businessman in Canada. He worked most with trade to the UK market. To move his goods, he bought a new boat. The boat was 290 tons. He named it "Sophie" after his wife. Then, he bought two other boats. He bought them all or part of them.

In 1832, a man named Masson helped start a train in Canada. This train was called the Champlain and St. Lawrence Railroad. Masson also put money in things for cities. These things were water to drink and gas lights. These places were Montreal, Quebec, and Toronto. His friends asked him to help in Montreal. So, he put money there. By 1842, he put in a lot of money. This was more than one-third of the société du Gaz de Montréal. He started a company in Quebec with John Strang. This company was for water and gas lights. He also started another company in Toronto with Furniss. This company was for lights, gas, and water. He was the boss of that company. The Banque de Montréal was like Canada's main bank. Masson already owned shares in the Bank of Canada. So, he bought shares of the Bank of Montreal in 1824. He joined the board of directors there in 1826. In 1830, Masson bought even more shares.

Masson was a businessman. He made much money. He used "credit." Credit means money that you get now, but you pay later. He used this to grow his business big. He doubled his business size. But, he had trouble. His Scottish partner could not agree to do the same. Masson became the main boss. He was also the big owner. The company names changed. They now had "Masson" in them. For example, Joseph Masson, Sons and Company. He was a big businessman in Canada in the 1830s. In 1832, Masson bought a land place. This land was called the seigneurie de Terrebonne. This land came from the old New France ways. He bought it for status. But, Masson worked hard. He wanted to earn much money from this. He grew trade and industry. He built forges and mills. He brought new ways of working.

Masson was in a group called the Legislative Council. This was for Lower Canada. He was there from 1834 to 1838. He was also in the Montreal City Council. This was starting in December 1842. People asked him to be the Mayor of Montreal. This was in December 1842. But he said no. He was a captain in the militia since 1823. He was also on the Board of Trade in Montreal. He was the first church leader for Notre-Dame parish. He was also a judge of the peace. Masson liked his business the most. He was usually loyal to the King. In 1837, he helped take loyalty promises in Montreal. But he was also a patriot. A patriot means someone who loves their country. He hid Louis-Joseph Papineau. This man had a reward on his head in 1837. Later, in 1845, he agreed to be the third president of the Association Saint-Jean-Baptiste.

Mr. Masson passed away in 1847 in Terrebonne. People think he was the first rich French-Canadian man. After he died, his wife, Marie-Geneviève-Sophie Masson, and her oldest sons kept Masson's businesses running. They also managed the seigneurie. She started the seigneurial bureau in 1850. She also built the Masson manor from 1848 to 1854. She grew the industry and the mills. She also started the Collège Masson in Terrebonne. They had twelve children. Coquitlam City Hall is a place. It is where the Coquitlam City Council meets. This is in Coquitlam, British Columbia, Canada. It sits where Pinetree Way and Guildford Way meet. The City Hall is part of two buildings. The other building is the Public Safety Building. This building holds the Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP) group.

Two big buildings were made by Grant & Sinclair Architects Limited. They finished them in the year 2001. One building is for the Coquitlam City Council. This big place has three floors. It is very big. It has rooms for the council. It also has city offices. And it has a public library. The Coquitlam Public Library moved to a new spot in 2013. This new spot is at 1169 Pinetree Way. This move gave them much more space. It made the library nearly three times bigger. Another big building is 67,800 square feet. This place holds the city's other groups. It also has an RCMP office. James J. Mutrie was a man. He was a big star in baseball. He helped start the New York Metropolitans and the New York Giants. He was the first boss for both teams. He had a winning rate of .611. This was the best for managers in the 1800s. It is still very high today.

Mutrie was a man. People called him "Smilin' Jeems" and "Truthful Jim." He was born in a place called Chelsea, Massachusetts. He grew up playing cricket. Later, he played baseball when he was sixteen years old. He played in the minor leagues from 1877 to 1879. In 1880, he moved from New England to New York. In New York, he got money help. The people who helped were August Belmont and John B. Day. They helped him to start a team. This team was called the independent New York Metropolitans. At the end of the 1882 season, Day and Mutrie got offers. The offers were from two groups. One was the American Association. The other was the National League. They wanted a New York team. They did both things. They joined the Mets in the American Association. Also, they took most players from the Troy Trojans. They used these players to make a team. This new team for the National League was called the New York Gothams.

Mutrie was a coach for the New York Metropolitans. He coached them from 1880 to 1882. In those years, he helped the team win two big cups. He won them in 1881 and also in 1882. In 1882, the Metropolitans played a lot. Their record was 101 wins, 57 losses, and 3 ties. They won the League Alliance cup easily. From 1880 to 1882, Mutrie helped the team have 201 wins. He also helped them have 136 losses and 7 ties. Mutrie kept coaching them in 1883 and 1884. In 1884, he led them to the World Series. In the 1884 World Series, Mutrie played against Frank Bancroft. Bancroft was a manager before. He managed the New Bedford Whalers in 1878. The Whalers won their league cup that year. In 1885, Mutrie started coaching the Gothams. He is known for giving them the name, the Giants. They had great players like Buck Ewing and Roger Connor.

Mutrie stopped playing baseball. He then ran a hotel in Elmira, New York. He also ran a newsstand on Staten Island. He passed away from cancer in New York City. He was 86 years old when he died. They buried him at the Moravian Cemetery on Staten Island. György Moldova was a writer. He lived from 1934 to 2022. He wrote more than seventy books in Hungary. These books sold over 13 million copies. This is more than any other writer from Hungary. He is famous for his books about real life. These books looked at everyday life. They focused on certain jobs or places. For example, he wrote about trains, digging in mines, and police work. He did deep research for these books. He used stories from his travels. He also used talks with people he met.

Moldova was a writer. His funny books were very popular. People in his home country gave him many awards. One award was the Kossuth Prize. He wrote plays and story books. One story book is called *Sötét angyal*, which means Dark Angel. This book came out in English in 1967. He also wrote a book about real life. It came out in 2012. The book was called *Ballpoint: A Tale of Genius and Grit*. Moldova lived in Budapest. When he was born, his name was György Reif. His parents were Jewish people. In 1944, they were put in a Jewish area in Budapest. They stayed there until World War II ended. Later, he studied at the College of Theatre and Drama in Budapest. He left in 1957. He did this before finishing his school. A friend and a teacher were arrested after the 1956 Revolution. Moldova did not get the award.

Moldova has written many things. He wrote books and plays for many years. He also wrote for a funny paper called Hócipő. This paper came out every two weeks in Budapest. In the early 1990s, he wrote for a daily paper. This paper was called Magyar Hirlap. Moldova writes about things in daily life. He writes about actions. He writes about people who are like simple, everyday heroes. His true writing is like a special kind of news writing. His non-fiction books are very popular. They talk about things that many people in Hungary care about. Even though some big writers do not like him, many people in Hungary like him. All kinds of people read his work. They like him even if they do not like his strong feelings.

People from other countries read about the ballpoint pen. Moldova made a book about it. The English book came out in 2012. People from outside Hungary read it. Henry Petroski liked the book. He said it reads like a fun mystery. He knew the ballpoint pen would win. But he was surprised by the story. The story had secrets. It had money tricks. It had legal troubles. Robert Friedel also read the book. He said the story of László Bíró and Andor Goy was wonderful. This story shows how people's feelings and smart ideas shape the world. These ideas made the ballpoint pen.

In the year 1988, Moldova made a book. The book was called Bűn az élet. This book looked at the real lives of police officers. It talked about something called cigánybűnözés. This meant crime by the Roma people. The Roma are a group of people. The word is now not liked by many people in Hungary. They say it has bad feelings. People said the book only showed bad things about Roma people. A lawyer named János Bársony said the book helped support bad feelings against Roma people. This was in the late 1980s. He said the book showed police like "Wild West heroes." These heroes protected people. A smart person named György Szerbhorváth said the government asked for the book. The Ministry of the Interior gave them information. This information later turned out to be wrong.

Moldova was a man. He said he was an "independent leftist." This means he did not belong to any political group. However, Moldova liked communist ideas. He was very loyal to János Kádár. Kádár was a leader in Hungary. He was a communist leader. He ruled Hungary from 1956 until 1988. Moldova wrote a book about Kádár. In this book, he called Kádár a "genius." He also said that punishing people after the 1956 Hungarian Revolution was okay. This page began as a copy. It was from a Hungarian Wikipedia page about György Moldova. This article is about a painting. The painting is from the 1770s. It needs more words. You can help Wikipedia by adding more things. Xavier Marcel Boulestin was a French chef. He did not find his job right away. He worked as a secretary. He also wrote things for another writer named "Willy." Later, he moved to London. He lived and worked there from 1906. He opened a shop for home looks. But the shop did not make much money. He wrote many books. People asked him to write a simple French cooking book for English people. This book was a big hit. Boulestin is a man. His restaurant was called Boulestin. It was very famous. It was known as the most costly place in London. The restaurant opened in the year 1927. He became a star. This was because of his food and the many books he wrote. His cooking was big. It had many kinds of food. He cooked French foods. He also cooked foods that British cooks knew. Elizabeth David, who was an English food writer, was one person who was helped by Boulestin. She wrote about him in her books. She also used many of his ideas. Boulestin was born in Poitiers, France. His mom and his grandma raised him there. His mom's side parents lived in different places. So, young Boulestin spent one month each summer with his dad in Saint-Aulaye. He learned in Poitiers. Later, he learned in Bordeaux. He was supposed to study law there. But really, he spent his time at music shows. He was part of the city's music life. He wrote "Letter from Bordeaux" for a music paper.

After army time in 1899, Boulestin went to Paris. He worked for a man named Willy. Boulestin was a secretary for Willy. He was also a ghostwriter. This means he wrote books for Willy. Willy wrote very popular books. Some friends of Willy were in his stories. His books showed Colette's young life. The stories had characters from different times. Two characters were "Hicksem" and "Blackspot." These characters came from Boulestin himself. In 1904, Willy wrote a book called *En Bombe*. This book showed Willy's life. It showed his life with Boulestin and other secretaries. It had 100 pictures. The pictures showed Willy as Maugis. They showed Boulestin as Blackspot. They showed another secretary as Kernadeck. They showed Colette as Marcelle. They also showed Colette's dog, Toby-Chien. Also in 1905, a French book came out. This book was Boulestin's translation. It was of *The Happy Hypocrite*. The book was in the Mercure de France. Max Beerbohm made a funny drawing of Boulestin.

Boulestin liked English things a lot. This was true even for food. He tried to show his family that mint sauce with mutton was good. He bought small sweet pies and marmalade in Paris. He also took a lady named Colette to tea in the afternoon. He moved to London in the year 1906. He lived and worked there after that. But he never thought about becoming a British citizen. A writer named Elizabeth David said that a Frenchman should not give up his home country. At first, he spent time in music places and big, fancy places. These places belonged to rich people who did not work. Some of his friends were Robert Ross, Lord Alfred Douglas, and Reginald Turner. First, he made money by writing funny letters. These letters were called "Letters from London." He wrote for many magazines. One magazine was Akademos. This magazine was a fancy monthly book. For Akademos, Boulestin also wrote a long story.

In November of 1911, a shop named Boulestin opened. This shop was for decorating the inside of houses. It was at 15 Elizabeth Street in London. Boulestin said his things were small. But they were new and very good. He did not change his style for anyone. He sold nice things like silks and velvets. He also sold linens and pretty small things. The wallpapers came from places like Martine and André Groult. He bought things from cities like Darmstadt and Munich. He got papers from Berlin. Paris gave him fun vases and nice cups. He also sold some fine art from Africa. Famous people visited his shop. Syrie Maugham, who would be a decorator, was a client. Lady Curzon and Mrs. Hwfa Williams were also his customers. The Countess of Drogheda also liked his shop. Princess Lichnowsky visited too. She helped make the Omega Workshops successful.

When the First World War started, Boulestin worked with the French army. He helped the British army. He worked as a speaker, or interpreter, for them. He also did something fun. He helped design the clothes for a famous army concert group. This group was called the "Rouges et Noirs." Sometimes, he made the British soldiers laugh. He taught them how to cook. After the war, he went back to London. He opened his design shop again at 102 George Street, Portman Square. But, the shop did not do well. He thought that maybe it was too new for people. Also, many shops were starting new kinds of decorations. Some women who used to buy from him were now decorating their own homes. Plus, things were going badly for shops. Sometimes, when someone gave him a big order, he could not finish it. This was because the cloth was hard to find.

Boulestin wrote a book. The book was called Keepsake. His friend, Jean Émile Laboureur, drew pictures for it. But, his money kept getting low. So, he found other ways to earn money. He taught French lessons. He also made candle covers by hand. He worked as a wine helper for people. Then, around 1923, someone asked him to write a cooking book. The book was for English homes. It was called Simple French Cooking for English Homes. It came out in June 1923. Everyone liked it right away. In England then, people did not like to talk about food. But Boulestin thought that good food is good to talk about. People liked this idea. His book sold so much. It was printed many times between 1923 and 1930.

In 1925, a man named Boulestin opened a nice place to eat. It was called The Restaurant Français. This restaurant was in Leicester Square in London. The architect made it. The decorator also made it. The chef was a Frenchman named M. Bigorre. He worked in Paris before. Later, in 1927, Boulestin moved. He moved to Southampton Street in Covent Garden. He opened the Restaurant Boulestin there. The new place had fun pictures. These pictures were about the circus. A man named Laboureur painted them. A French artist named Marie Laurencin painted too. Raoul Dufy made the cloth. Cecil Beaton said it was the prettiest restaurant in London. Another book called The Restaurants of London said it was a modern Parisian place. It was also a very fancy place. The carpet was wine color. The curtains had yellow patterns. Over the fireplace, there was a painting of a dinner table.

People thought Boulestin's restaurant was very good. A writer named Edward Laroque Tinker said this in The New York Times. He said that at Boulestin's, you can find the best and most special dinner in all of London. The rules at Boulestin were very strict. Even though it was said to be the most costly restaurant in London, the Restaurant Boulestin did not make money. So, the owner had to earn more money by writing many articles and books. He wrote some of these with Arthur Henry "Robin" Adair. Robin Adair was a food writer from Britain. He became Boulestin's helper and writing friend in 1923. Elizabeth David was also inspired by Boulestin's writing. After he passed away, she became the main writer in Britain about food. In her books, she used words from Boulestin's writings. One of those writings was from a book called What Shall We Have Today?: "The chief thing."

Boulestin was a TV cook. He was the first regular TV cook. He cooked for the BBC. This was in the early days of TV. It was between 1937 and 1939. In his shows, he cooked many foods. He cooked fancy French dishes. For example, he made Escalope de Veau Choisy. He also cooked simple foods. These included salads and lamb kebabs. In the summer of 1939, Boulestin and Adair went on holiday. Boulestin built a house there. When Germany came to France, it was hard. Adair was sick. He could not leave. Boulestin stayed with him. The Germans kept Adair. They held him in a place called Bayonne. Later, they held him near Paris. Boulestin moved to Paris. He wanted to be close to Adair. Boulestin passed away there. He was 65 years old.

Adair came back to England after the war. He became the cookery writer for a big magazine called Harper's Bazaar. He passed away in 1956. Boulestin's restaurant kept going. Many people managed it until 1994. Joel Kissin opened a cafe and restaurant in London. He named it after Boulestin. He also opened his own restaurant in Covent Garden in 2013. Thomas was a very important man in England. He was a nobleman. His name was Thomas, 2nd Earl of Lancaster. He lived a long time ago. He was part of the Plantagenet family. This family was like a royal line. He held many titles. He was the Earl of Lancaster, Leicester, and Derby. He had these titles from 1296 until he died in 1322. He also held other titles. These were Earl of Lincoln and Salisbury. These were from 1311 to 1322. Thomas was a very strong baron. He was a big leader. He was against his cousin, King Edward II. Thomas was the first son of Edmund Crouchback. His mother was Blanche of Artois. She was the Queen Dowager of Navarre. Edmund Crouchback was a son of King Henry III of England. Because of his mother, Thomas was also a half-brother to Queen Joan I of Navarre.

His marriage to Alice de Lacy was not happy. They did not have any children together. But he had two sons. These sons were named John and Thomas. In the year 1317, someone took Alice from her big house. This happened at Canford, in Dorset. A knight named Richard de St Martin took her. He worked for John de Warenne. He was the 7th Earl of Surrey. This bad thing made two big families fight. The Lancasters and the Surreys fought. The Lancasters took two castles from the Surreys. This was their way of fighting back. King Edward then helped them. The two earls made a shaky peace. Thomas kept the big titles of Lincoln and Salisbury. This was because of a promise between the two families. When his father-in-law died, Thomas would keep these titles. He would keep them for himself. This was not how people thought it would happen.

When he grew up, he became the sheriff of Lancashire. This job was for his family. But he spent ten years fighting in Scotland for Edward I. So, other helpers looked after the sheriff job. He was at the Battle of Falkirk in 1298. He was with Edward I's army. He was also there for the crowning of his cousin, King Edward II of England. This was on February 25, 1308. He carried a special sword called Curtana. At first, the people of Lancaster liked Edward. But the fighting between the king and the big lords kept going. Because of this, Lancaster changed his mind. He did not like a favorite person named Piers Gaveston. Gaveston made fun of him. He called him "the Fiddler." He also made him promise revenge. This happened when Gaveston told the king to fire one of Lancaster's helpers.

Lancaster was a powerful lord. He wanted to send Gaveston away. He also wanted the lords to have more power. Lancaster had his own army. This army helped take the king and Gaveston apart. Lancaster was one of the judges. These judges found Gaveston guilty. They saw him die in the year 1312. After a big fight at Bannockburn in 1314, Edward listened to Lancaster. This meant Lancaster was like the boss of England. He tried to rule for four years. But he could not keep things calm. The Scots kept raiding and taking back land in the North. In 1318, the lords did not like him so much. They asked him to accept less power. A new group of leaders took charge. They were Hugh le Despenser. His son was Hugh Despenser the Younger. The lords did not like this new group either. So, in 1321, Lancaster started a fight again. This time, he was the leader of the fight.

Lancaster went to a big court. Many important people were there. These people were the Despensers. Edmund Fitzalan, the 9th Earl of Arundel, and King Edward were there too. Lancaster could not speak. Nobody else could speak for him. He was found guilty of treason. This means he betrayed his country. He was given the death sentence. Because of his family and his royal blood, the king changed the death. Instead of being hanged, drawn, and beheaded, he would just be beheaded. Lancaster died on March 22, 1322. This happened near Pontefract Castle. After he died, his titles and land were taken away. The Scots took this chance. They wanted Lancaster's things. This happened in the Great Raid of 1322. Later, in 1323, his little brother Henry asked to have the earldom of Leicester. In 1326 or 1327, the Parliament changed this.

After Thomas passed away, people saw magic things at his grave in Pontefract. Because of this, people treated him like a saint. In the year 1327, a group asked King Edward III to make him a saint. People kept honoring him until a big change happened called the reformation. Then, in 1822, people said they found Thomas's bones. They found them in a big stone box. It was shown with another story about a "fossil ship." After that, the story went away.
